extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim launched an early sprint from distance to hold off NIZZOLO Giacomo and VIVIANI Elia in Novara bunch finish.
tactical_patterns: GANNA Filippo took intermediate sprint bonus seconds despite wearing rosa, aggressively defending GC position rather than conserving energy.
MERLIER Tim's long-range sprint strategy succeeded where typical late acceleration failed; sustained power trumped positioning in chaotic finale.
GAVIRIA Fernando went wrong side of leadout MOLANO forcing barrier-side finish; Deceuninck leadout discipline fractured under pressure.
GROENEWEGEN Dylan's fourth place shows race fitness lacking top-end speed after nine-month absence; avoid backing early-season returns.

rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: positive: first Grand Tour stage win executing risky long sprint strategy, shows confidence in sustained power over positioning
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: aggressively took intermediate sprint bonus seconds in rosa, moved to fourth GC, treating flat stage tactically not ceremonially
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: neutral: perfect positioning on MERLIER wheel but lacked final acceleration to come around, execution without penetration
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: had SABATINI leadout but hesitated at critical moment when MERLIER launched, decisiveness issue not legs
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: navigational error bypassing leadout wrong side shows positional awareness problem, barriers saved crash not skill
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: fourth after nine-month layoff confirms top-end speed deficit persists, positioning good but acceleration blunted
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: tenth with poor positioning throughout suggests team support collapsed or personal awareness failed in chaos
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: fifth after weaving through traffic demonstrates salvage skills but starting position inadequate for pure sprinter role
